reasoning: All three reach the same sound diagnosis (a residency/presence problem misdiagnosed as retail) and similar remedies, so differentiation is in reasoning quality. B wins on explicit metacognition (names the substitution heuristic, Meadows leverage points, broken-windows and success-to-the-successful loops, bounded-rationality equilibrium), the best calibration (~80% on diagnosis, lower on remedies, names what the brief cannot resolve like ownership concentration determining which lever works), and red-teaming its own Option B. A edges C with concrete quantified reasoning (worker-trip multipliers and membership math, each flagged as illustrative) and crisp instrumented test designs with clear metrics. C is clean and actionable with a sharp coordination-failure reframe, but it surfaces fewer cognitive traps and expresses uncertainty more thinly than B.
